skill.name: anndata
skill.description: Guidance for working with AnnData objects, h5ad files, and annotated matrices in Python single-cell analysis workflows.
skill.summary: Work with AnnData, h5ad, zarr, and single-cell metadata patterns in Python.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, weak-crypto, C2, and network patterns, but manual review found documentation examples rather than executable skill code. The skill contains markdown guidance for AnnData, including install commands and optional remote data examples, so the remaining risk is low and user-controlled.

low_findings.title: Markdown examples misclassified as command execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are fenced documentation examples, including package installation and Python AnnData snippets. No script or skill runtime executes these commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are markdown code fences and explanatory examples. I found no executable scripts, hooks, or automation that would run those commands without user action.

low_findings.title: Optional remote data examples require user judgment
low_findings.description: The network findings are examples for S3, URL-backed Zarr, URL downloads, and public documentation links. They do not include credential handling or hidden exfiltration behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are example.com placeholders or public project documentation. The urllib example downloads the same user-specified dataset URL to a local h5ad file.

low_findings.title: Security keyword detections are semantic false positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, C2, system reconnaissance, and certificate detections appear to match ordinary scientific data terms such as HDF5, keys, descriptions, and metadata inspection examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic APIs, C2 endpoints, credential material, or host reconnaissance logic. The matches are documentation text and AnnData field names.

content.value_statement: Single-cell analysis needs consistent handling of annotated matrices, metadata, and large files. This skill gives practical AnnData guidance for Python workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains AnnData object structure, including X, obs, var, layers, obsm, varm, obsp, varp, uns, and raw.
Shows how to read and write h5ad, zarr, CSV, MTX, Loom, 10X, Excel, and text formats.
Guides concatenation across observations or variables with joins, merge strategies, labels, and batch keys.
Covers subsetting, filtering, copying, transposition, renaming, type conversion, and data reordering patterns.
Provides memory and performance guidance for sparse matrices, backed mode, chunked processing, and categoricals.
Connects AnnData workflows with Scanpy, Muon, scvi-tools, and other scverse ecosystem tools.
limitations: It is documentation guidance and does not ship executable analysis scripts or validation tools.
It does not replace domain review of biological quality control or experimental design choices.
Remote data examples require users to verify source trust, access permissions, and file integrity.
Examples may need adjustment for specific AnnData, Scanpy, or dependency versions.

use_cases.title: Prepare single-cell datasets
use_cases.description: Create AnnData objects, attach cell and gene metadata, store raw counts, and write reproducible h5ad files.

use_cases.title: Optimize large matrix workflows
use_cases.description: Use sparse matrices, backed mode, zarr storage, and chunked access for datasets that exceed memory limits.

use_cases.title: Combine experimental batches
use_cases.description: Concatenate samples with clear batch labels, align indices, choose joins, and preserve useful metadata.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic AnnData object
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a matrix and metadata table.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create an AnnData object from my expression matrix, cell metadata, and gene metadata. Explain each component in simple terms.

prompt_templates.title: Read and inspect an h5ad file
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through loading an h5ad file, checking its dimensions, reviewing obs and var columns, and identifying memory risks.

prompt_templates.title: Merge batches safely
prompt_templates.prompt: I have several AnnData objects from different batches. Recommend a concatenation strategy with labels, keys, joins, and metadata checks.

prompt_templates.title: Design a scalable AnnData pipeline
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for large single-cell datasets.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a memory-aware AnnData workflow for large h5ad and zarr datasets, including backed mode, sparse matrices, chunking, and reproducibility practices.

output_examples.input: User asks how to inspect a new h5ad file before analysis.
output_examples.output: The assistant outlines a review plan: load metadata first, check dimensions, inspect obs and var fields, verify sparsity, and choose backed mode when needed.

output_examples.input: User asks how to combine three batches without losing source labels.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends concatenating observations with explicit batch labels, unique keys, an appropriate join, and index checks before downstream correction.

output_examples.input: User reports memory errors while processing a large single-cell matrix.
output_examples.output: The assistant suggests backed mode, sparse matrices, categorical metadata, chunked processing, and selective loading of filtered subsets.

best_practices: Use sparse matrices and categorical metadata when data is sparse or labels repeat often.
Store raw counts before filtering or normalization so downstream comparisons remain reproducible.
Track batch, sample, and dataset labels explicitly during concatenation and integration workflows.
anti_patterns: Do not load full h5ad files into memory when backed mode can answer the immediate question.
Do not concatenate datasets without preserving source labels and checking index uniqueness.
Do not assign external metadata by row order when stable observation or variable IDs are available.

faq.question: What is AnnData used for?
faq.answer: AnnData stores a data matrix with aligned observation metadata, variable metadata, annotations, and derived analysis results.

faq.question: Does this skill run analysis automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples for building AnnData workflows in Python.

faq.question: Can it help with large h5ad files?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains backed mode, sparse matrices, zarr storage, chunking, and memory-aware access patterns.

faq.question: Does it cover Scanpy workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes AnnData patterns used by Scanpy preprocessing, dimensionality reduction, clustering, and visualization workflows.

faq.question: Can it combine multiple datasets?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers AnnData concatenation, batch labels, join choices, merge strategies, and index conflict handling.

faq.question: What should users verify before downloading remote data?
faq.answer: Users should verify the source, permissions, expected format, file size, and integrity before loading remote datasets.
